zh.lifeSummary: Richard Hoord Of Walrod and Maisters是Henry De Hoord的兒子，於1242年出生。他和Jocosa Young至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Richard Hoord Of Walrod and Maisters是Henry De Hoord的儿子，于1242年出生。他和Jocosa Young至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: Hoord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Middlesex): variant of   Board  .

variants.name: Board
meanings: English: occupational name or nickname from Middle English bord(e) ‘board, plank, table’, denoting a carpenter or sawyer, a maker of boards and planks, or perhaps a nickname for a tall, thin person.

nameMeanings.name: Of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese (Ōi): written 大井 ‘large well’. It is found in central and eastern Japan.
Japanese (Ōi): written 多 ‘many’. It was pronounced Oho in ancient times and is listed in the Shinsen shōjiroku. It is not common in modern Japan.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 黃, see   Huang   1.

variants.name: Huang
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Wei, Ming, Jian, Ying, Yi, Jing, Wen, Jin, Ping, Min, Yong, Cheng, Hong, Chong, Chang, Pao, Sha, Shen, Tun, You, Chung, Shiu, Ti, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 黄, meaning ‘yellow’ in Chinese: (i) from Huang (黃), the name of an ancient state (present-day Huangchuan in Henan province)annexed by the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) traced back to some minority ethnic groups in ancient southern China.

nameMeanings.name: Walrod
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Walrath  , a surname of German origin.

variants.name: Walrath
meanings: Americanized form of German   Wallrath  .

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from a pet form of the personal names   Andrew   or   Andreas  .
Native American (mainly Yakama): adoption of the English personal name Andy (see 1 above) as a surname.

variants.name: Andrew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the usual vernacular English form (recorded from the 13th century onward) of the New Testament Greek personal name Andreas . In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Czech Ondřej (see Ondrey ) and Albanian Ndreu , and also their patronymics and other derivatives (see examples at Andrews ).
History: The surname Andrew was first brought to North America from England by Robert Andrew (died 1668), who settled in Boxford, MA.

variants.name: Endy
meanings: Perhaps a variant of   Andy  .

nameMeanings.name: Maisters
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of Masters . Marsters is the predominant surname on Palmerston Island, Cook Islands, South Pacific, deriving from a settler and owner reputedly born in Leicestershire.

variants.name: Masters
meanings: habitational and occupational name from Middle English (atte) maisteres ‘(at the) master's (house)’, a name for someone who lived or worked there, normally a servant. Compare Master and Masterman , and for the type compare Maidens , Monks , Parsons .
variant of Master , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
English:

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

en.lifeSummary: Richard Hoord Of Walrod and Maisters was born in 1242 as the son of Henry De Hoord. He had at least 1 son with Jocosa Young.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard Hoord Of Walrod and Maisters est né en 1242, fils de Henry De Hoord. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Jocosa Young.

de.lifeSummary: Richard Hoord Of Walrod and Maisters wurde 1242, als Sohn von Henry De Hoord geboren. Er hatte mit Jocosa Young mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Richard Hoord Of Walrod and Maisters è nato il nel 1242 come figlio di Henry De Hoord. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Jocosa Young.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Hoord Of Walrod and Maistersは1242年にHenry De Hoordの息子として生まれた。彼はJocosa Youngとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Hoord Of Walrod and Maisters(은)는 1242년 Henry De Hoord의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Jocosa Young(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Richard Hoord Of Walrod and Maisters nasceu em 1242 filho de Henry De Hoord. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Jocosa Young.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard Hoord Of Walrod and Maisters родился в 1242 г. Его отца звали Henry De Hoord. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Jocosa Young.

es.lifeSummary: Richard Hoord Of Walrod and Maisters nació en 1242 como hijo de Henry De Hoord. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Jocosa Young.
